framework: A path qualifies only if you can point at companies hiring for it or a role title that demonstrably exists - not a blog asserting it matters. Ranked by strength of that evidence, not by how often the skill is praised. Curated by hand; this file is editorial, which is why it is NOT inside <role>.json (that bundle is DOL-derived only).

paths.what_it_is: Run the analysis and build the model, rather than briefing someone else to do it.
paths.why_it_signals: Every major firm built a named unit for this: McKinsey QuantumBlack (~1,700 people, 40+ offices), BCG X (3,000+ technologists), Bain's Advanced Analytics Group embedded directly in case teams.

paths.path: Ship a working thing, not only a recommendation
paths.what_it_is: Build and deploy something the client actually runs, rather than delivering the deck that describes it.
paths.why_it_signals: BCG X is defined by exactly this distinction: it builds and deploys production-ready software, not just analytical models.

paths.what_it_is: Own a named industry rather than positioning as a generalist problem-solver.
paths.why_it_signals: Firms organise by practice, and across hiring generally, demonstrated depth in a domain now beats generalist positioning.
